description:
A configuration-manager wants to keep daily, version-stamped backups of all
  application configuration files that live under  
  /home/user/configs.  
  Three items already exist in that directory tree and MUST NOT be modified
  by your script:

    1. /home/user/configs/app.conf  
    2. /home/user/configs/db.yml  
    3. /home/user/configs/nginx/nginx.conf

  Goal  
  ----  
  1. Create a new directory /home/user/backups (if it does not yet exist).  
  2. Create a **separate tar.gz archive for each of the three configuration
     files**.  All three archives have to be launched in parallel (background
     jobs) and stored inside /home/user/backups.  
       • Naming pattern: <stem>_20230930.tar.gz  
         – app.conf  →  app_20230930.tar.gz  
         – db.yml    →  db_20230930.tar.gz  
         – nginx.conf→  nginx_20230930.tar.gz  
       • Each archive must keep the *relative* path inside the tarball so
         that a restore returns the file to its original location beneath
         /home/user/configs.  
  3. After all three background jobs complete, create one additional,
     top-level archive called  
       /home/user/backups/configs_full_20230930.tar.gz  
     containing the entire /home/user/configs directory tree (again preserving
     relative paths).  
  4. Generate a machine-verifiable log file
       /home/user/backup_logs/backup_20230930.log  
     with the exact, **three-column, pipe-delimited** layout shown below.
     The file must end with a single newline and MUST be sorted
     lexicographically (ASCII order) by the first column:

       absolute_path_to_original_file|absolute_path_to_created_archive|sha256sum_of_original_file_contents

     For example (DO **NOT** hard-code the checksums, compute them):

       /home/user/configs/app.conf|/home/user/backups/app_20230930.tar.gz|7b4639...
       /home/user/configs/db.yml|/home/user/backups/db_20230930.tar.gz|3e1d44...
       /home/user/configs/nginx/nginx.conf|/home/user/backups/nginx_20230930.tar.gz|9c055e...

     Requirements for the log:
       • Exactly three lines (one per original file).  
       • Columns are separated by a single “|” (pipe) character,
         no extra spaces.  
       • The checksum column must match the value returned by
         “sha256sum <file>” on the original file, *not* on the archive.  
       • A final trailing newline is mandatory.  
  5. The script must return to the shell prompt with a zero exit status.

  Hints  
  -----  
  • You may find “tar -czf”, “sha256sum”, background execution “&”, and
    “wait” useful.  
  • Verify that all four *.tar.gz files exist and are non-empty before
    writing the log.  
  • Make sure /home/user/backup_logs exists (create it if necessary) and that
    the log file is written with default user permissions so the
    automated tests can read it.

  What will be checked
  --------------------  
  1. Presence of four archives in /home/user/backups with the exact names
     specified above.  
  2. Each archive must unpack to precisely the expected file(s) with the
     correct relative paths.  
  3. The log file must exist at the exact path, be world-readable, contain
     exactly three lines, be pipe-delimited, and end with a newline.  
  4. For every line in the log the automated test will:
        a. Confirm that the path in column-1 exists and is one of the
           three known config files.  
        b. Confirm that the archive named in column-2 exists.  
        c. Independently compute the SHA-256 digest of the original file and
           check it matches column-3.  

  DO NOT simply paste commands here; execute them in the terminal so the
  resulting files appear on disk.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing directory layout and file contents.  These objects are
       created for the agent before the task begins and must remain
       unmodified. -->
  <preexisting>
    /home/user/configs/            (0755, owned by user)
    /home/user/configs/app.conf    (0644, owned by user)
    /home/user/configs/db.yml      (0644, owned by user)
    /home/user/configs/nginx/      (0755, owned by user)
    /home/user/configs/nginx/nginx.conf (0644, owned by user)
  </preexisting>

  <!-- Exact file contents so the grading harness can calculate digests -->
  <file_contents>
    <file path="/home/user/configs/app.conf"><![CDATA[
# Application configuration
port=8080
debug=true
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/configs/db.yml"><![CDATA[
database:
  host: localhost
  port: 5432
  user: app
  password: secret
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/configs/nginx/nginx.conf"><![CDATA[
user www-data;
worker_processes auto;
events { worker_connections 1024; }
http {
    server {
        listen 80;
        location / {
            proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:8080;
        }
    }
}
]]></file>
  </file_contents>

  <!-- Directories the agent must create -->
  <agent_created>
    /home/user/backups/            (0755, owned by user)
    /home/user/backup_logs/        (0755, owned by user)
  </agent_created>

  <!-- Files that must exist after task completion -->
  <expected_output>
    /home/user/backups/app_20230930.tar.gz            (non-empty)
    /home/user/backups/db_20230930.tar.gz             (non-empty)
    /home/user/backups/nginx_20230930.tar.gz          (non-empty)
    /home/user/backups/configs_full_20230930.tar.gz   (non-empty)

    /home/user/backup_logs/backup_20230930.log        (0644, size > 0)
  </expected_output>
